seoTitle: How to Delete a Customer in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Remove duplicate or inactive customers from QuickBooks Online to keep your customer list clean and your financial records accurate.
intro: As your customer list grows, duplicate entries or clients who've stopped doing business with you can clutter your records. This guide explains how and when to delete a customer in QuickBooks Online, so your accounts stay organized whether you're a small business owner or an accountant managing multiple client files.

steps.heading: Review your customer list
steps.body: Go through your customers in QuickBooks Online to identify duplicates, inactive clients, or incorrect entries.

steps.heading: Confirm deletion is appropriate
steps.body: Check whether the customer has any outstanding transactions, since that can affect whether deletion is possible or advisable.

steps.heading: Delete or make the customer inactive
steps.body: Use QuickBooks' options to either delete the customer entirely or mark them inactive if you want to preserve historical transaction data.

steps.heading: Verify the change took effect
steps.body: Check your customer list afterward to confirm the entry was removed or archived as intended.

steps.heading: Keep your customer list maintained
steps.body: Periodically review your customer records so duplicates and outdated entries don't build up over time.

faq.question: Can I delete a customer with past transactions?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online often restricts deleting customers with transaction history; marking them inactive is usually the safer option in that case.

faq.question: What's the difference between deleting and making a customer inactive?
faq.answer: Deleting removes the entry outright, while making a customer inactive hides them from active lists but preserves their transaction history.

faq.question: Why would I need to delete a customer?
faq.answer: Common reasons include removing duplicate entries, cleaning up incorrect data, or removing clients who are no longer active.

faq.question: Does this affect my financial reports?
faq.answer: It can, particularly if the customer has associated transactions, so it's worth reviewing your reports after making changes.
